file_insights.primary_purpose: Dependency Injection container setup using tsyringe library
key_components: Service registration helpers (registerSingleton, registerInstance, registerFactory, registerScoped)
Child container management for request scoping
Re-exports of tsyringe decorators and types
exports: Re-exports: injectable, inject, singleton, delay, injectAll, DependencyContainer, InjectionToken

patterns_discovered.description: Uses string-based injection tokens with TypeScript type safety via InjectionToken<T> generic
patterns_discovered.implementation: TOKENS object maps readable names to strongly-typed tokens
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type-safe dependency injection with readable token names

patterns_discovered.description: Core infrastructure (Prisma, Redis) registered as singletons with existence checks
patterns_discovered.implementation: Uses container.isRegistered() check before registration to prevent duplicates
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures single instance across application lifetime

patterns_discovered.description: Provides multiple ways to register services (singleton, instance, factory, scoped)
patterns_discovered.implementation: Four helper functions with different registration approaches
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexibility for different dependency types (classes, instances, factories, scoped)
Isolates per-request dependencies and prevents cross-request pollution

patterns_discovered.description: resolveOptional with try-catch for optional dependencies
patterns_discovered.implementation: Returns undefined instead of throwing when dependency not found
patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows optional feature toggling without container errors

patterns_discovered.description: Detailed JSDoc comments with examples for every exported function
patterns_discovered.implementation: Module docstring, parameter descriptions, return types, @example blocks
patterns_discovered.benefit: Excellent developer experience and API discoverability

gotchas_discovered.description: Must import 'reflect-metadata' at top of file for tsyringe decorators to work
gotchas_discovered.impact: HIGH - Without this import, decorator metadata won't be available at runtime
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Already implemented as first import in the file

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Pre-created instances for Prisma and Redis
gotchas_discovered.description: Uses pre-imported prisma and redis instances rather than creating new ones
gotchas_discovered.impact: MEDIUM - Assumes proper initialization in @/config/database and @/config/redis
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Dependency injection handles this but requires config modules to be set up first

gotchas_discovered.description: registerScoped function doesn't truly implement scoped lifetime (noted in comment)
gotchas_discovered.impact: MEDIUM - Uses child containers instead for proper request scoping
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Function still present but comment explains the workaround

gotchas_discovered.description: disposeChildContainer must be called explicitly to prevent memory leaks
gotchas_discovered.impact: HIGH - Forgetting cleanup will accumulate memory over requests
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should be integrated into request lifecycle middleware

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple eslint-disable-next-line directives for console.log and @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
gotchas_discovered.impact: LOW - Intentional exceptions but indicates deliberate rule violations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Console for debugging, any type for flexibility in registration

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully implemented a comprehensive DI container setup using tsyringe with strong TypeScript integration
key_achievements: Created well-structured injection token system with type safety
Implemented multiple service registration strategies (singleton, instance, factory, scoped)
Provided intuitive resolution APIs (resolve, resolveOptional, isRegistered)
Added request-scoped dependency isolation via child containers
Included testing utilities for container reset
Proper decorator and type re-exports for consumer convenience
quality_indicators: Clear separation of concerns (tokens, registration, resolution, scoping)

recommendations.recommendation: Ensure registerCoreServices() is called during application bootstrap before module loading
recommendations.rationale: Core services (Prisma, Redis) must be available before any module tries to resolve them

recommendations.recommendation: Create request middleware that integrates createChildContainer and disposeChildContainer
recommendations.rationale: Manual cleanup will be error-prone; should be automatic in middleware lifecycle

recommendations.recommendation: Implement ModuleLoader service to dynamically register services from feature modules
recommendations.rationale: TOKENS.ModuleLoader is defined but not implemented; needed for dynamic feature registration

recommendations.recommendation: Add error handling wrapper around resolve() that provides context about missing dependencies
recommendations.rationale: Will improve debugging when services aren't registered as expected

recommendations.recommendation: Create test helper that combines resetContainer() and registerCoreServices() into single function
recommendations.rationale: Tests will repeatedly perform this sequence; helper will reduce boilerplate

recommendations.recommendation: Add architectural diagram or guide explaining when to use singleton vs scoped vs factory
recommendations.rationale: While examples are clear, decision tree would help developers choose right pattern
